attractions.name: Casela Nature Parks
attractions.description: Wildlife and adventure park blending safari-style encounters with high-adrenaline activities

attractions.name: Black River Gorges National Park
attractions.description: National park with hiking trails, birdwatching spots, and rare flora
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

attractions.name: Alexandra Falls Viewpoint
attractions.description: Stunning lookout over lush ravines and cascading falls
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

attractions.name: La Vallée Des Couleurs Nature Park
attractions.description: Volcanic landscapes, 23-Coloured Earth, adventure rides (zipline, quad)
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

attractions.name: Blue Safari Submarine
attractions.description: Submarine tour descending 35 meters beneath Indian Ocean to witness coral reefs and shipwrecks

attractions.name: Ile aux Cerfs
attractions.description: Island with snorkeling, sunbathing, parasailing, beach BBQ lunch

attractions.name: Casela World of Adventures
attractions.description: Wildlife park with safari bus tours, big cat interactions, and adventure activities

attractions.name: L'Aventure du Sucre
attractions.description: Explore sugar production history & sample local rums

attractions.name: Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Botanical Garden
attractions.description: Known for giant water lilies & spice trees

attractions.name: Blue Safari Submarine Tour
attractions.description: Descend 35 meters into the Indian Ocean to view coral reefs and tropical marine life

attractions.name: Frangipani Beach
attractions.description: Beach at Shangri-La Le Touessrok resort

local_dishes.name: Dholl Puri
local_dishes.description: Thin, soft flatbreads made from ground split peas typically served in pairs, filled with a flavourful bean curry (gros pois), a tangy tomato chutney (satini pomme d'amour), and sometimes pickled vegetables (achard)
local_dishes.where_to_try: Street vendors in markets and near bus stations
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Roti (Farata)
local_dishes.description: Popular flatbread, often thicker and flakier than dholl puri. Served with various curries (meat, fish, or vegetable) and chutneys
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Gateaux Piments (Chili Cakes)
local_dishes.description: Crispy, deep-fried lentil cakes infused with fresh chilies and herbs like coriander and spring onions. They offer a spicy kick
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Mine Frites (Fried Noodles)
local_dishes.description: Stir-fried egg noodles with vegetables, soy sauce, and often chicken, shrimp, or beef
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Fish Vindaye
local_dishes.description: A tangy and flavourful fish curry marinated in mustard seeds, vinegar, garlic, ginger, and turmeric. Often made with tuna or other firm white fish
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Briani
local_dishes.description: A fragrant mixed rice dish cooked with meat (chicken, lamb, or fish), yogurt, saffron, and aromatic spices. Often served during special occasions and celebrations
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Palm Heart Salad (Salade de Palmiste)
local_dishes.description: Delicacy made from the tender heart of the palm tree with delicate, slightly nutty flavour
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Alouda
local_dishes.description: A refreshing milky drink flavoured with agar-agar jelly, rose syrup, and sometimes basil seeds or vanilla
source_files: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

local_dishes.name: Boulettes (Dumplings)
local_dishes.description: Steamed or boiled dumplings made with fish, shrimp, or vegetables, served with flavorful broth and chili sauce

local_dishes.name: Gateaux Piments
local_dishes.description: Crispy, deep-fried lentil cakes infused with fresh chilies and herbs

local_dishes.name: Samoosas
local_dishes.description: Triangular pastries filled with spiced vegetables, meat, or fish

local_dishes.name: Bajia
local_dishes.description: Fritters made from various vegetables coated in spiced chickpea flour batter and deep-fried until golden brown

local_dishes.name: Rougaille
local_dishes.description: A tomato-based stew, often featuring sausages (saucisse), salt fish (poisson salé), or seafood. Spiced with garlic, ginger, onions, and thyme and served with rice

local_dishes.name: Roti Chaud
local_dishes.description: A warm roti filled with curries, chutneys, and pickles — similar to dholl puri but thicker and more filling

local_dishes.name: Mine Frite (Mauritian Fried Noodles)
local_dishes.description: Chinese-inspired noodles stir-fried with vegetables, egg, and sometimes chicken or seafood

local_dishes.name: Boulettes (Mauritian Dumplings)
local_dishes.description: Steamed dumplings made of fish, chayote, or tofu served in a light broth

local_dishes.name: Octopus Curry (Cari Ourite)
local_dishes.description: A rich, flavorful octopus curry simmered with spices and coconut milk

local_dishes.name: Gateau Piment
local_dishes.description: Mauritian chilli cakes made from seasoned split peas — crispy and spicy

local_dishes.name: Napolitaine
local_dishes.description: Two soft shortbread biscuits sandwiched with strawberry jam and covered in pink icing

local_dishes.name: Puri Bhaji
local_dishes.description: Indian-inspired deep-fried puris served with mildly spiced mashed potato curry

local_dishes.name: Kulfi
local_dishes.description: Dense and creamy frozen dessert flavored with mango, cardamom, or pistachio

local_dishes.name: Mauritian Curries
local_dishes.description: Coconut-based, tomato-spiced, or dry-roasted styles using prawns, chicken, or octopus

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of your surroundings and secure your valuables, especially in crowded areas like markets and bus stations
safety_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid walking alone at night in unlit or isolated areas. Stick to well-lit and populated areas after dark
safety_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious with strangers who approach with unsolicited offers and avoid going to unfamiliar places with people you've just met
safety_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Never leave drinks unattended and be cautious about accepting drinks from strangers
safety_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Carry copies of important documents separate from originals and store digital copies securely
safety_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of your surroundings and secure your valuables, especially in crowded areas like markets and tourist hotspots
Avoid walking alone at night in unlit or isolated areas. Stick to well-lit and populated areas after dark
Be cautious with strangers and avoid going to unfamiliar places with people you've just met
Never leave drinks unattended and be cautious about accepting drinks from strangers
Always ask permission before taking someone's photograph, especially in local villages or during religious ceremonies
Be aware of your surroundings and secure your valuables, especially in crowded areas like markets and tourist hotspots
Avoid walking alone at night in unlit or isolated areas. Stick to well-lit and populated areas after dark
Never leave drinks unattended and be cautious about accepting drinks from strangers
Always ask permission before taking someone's photograph, especially in local villages or during religious ceremonies
Be aware of your surroundings and pay attention to belongings, especially in crowded areas like markets, bus stations, and tourist hotspots
Avoid displaying large amounts of cash or expensive jewellery. Keep valuables secure in hotel safe or concealed money belt
Carry copies of important documents and keep them separate from originals
Avoid walking alone at night in unlit or isolated areas. Stick to well-lit and populated areas after dark
Be cautious with strangers and avoid going to unfamiliar places with people you've just met
Never leave drinks unattended and be cautious about accepting drinks from strangers
Keep valuables secure and out of sight in busy areas
Use a money belt or neck pouch for passports and extra cash
Carry copies of travel documents (digital and paper)
Avoid isolated alleys or beaches late at night
In mosques/churches: dress modestly and be respectful
Be aware of taxi overcharging and no meter scams
Watch out for helpful guides offering free assistance
Be cautious of beach vendors overcharging
Be aware of your surroundings and pay attention to your belongings, especially in crowded areas like markets, bus stations, and tourist hotspots
Secure your valuables and avoid displaying large amounts of cash or expensive jewellery. Keep valuables in a hotel safe or concealed money belt
Carry copies of important documents and keep photocopies of passport, visa, flight tickets, and travel insurance details separate from originals
Avoid walking alone at night in unlit or isolated areas. Stick to well-lit and populated areas after dark
Be cautious with strangers and wary of overly friendly strangers with unsolicited offers. Avoid going to unfamiliar places with people you've just met
Protect your drinks - never leave drinks unattended and be cautious about accepting drinks from strangers to prevent drink spiking
Be aware of your surroundings, especially in crowded areas like markets, bus stations, and tourist hotspots
Secure your valuables and avoid displaying large amounts of cash or expensive jewellery
Carry copies of important documents and keep originals secure
Avoid walking alone at night in unlit or isolated areas
Be cautious with strangers and avoid going to unfamiliar places with people you've just met
Never leave drinks unattended and be cautious about accepting drinks from strangers

emergency_contacts.notes: High Commission of India, Port Louis

connectivity_tips.tip: Best providers are My.T (Mauritius Telecom), Emtel, and CHILI
connectivity_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Prepaid SIMs available at MRU airport or supermarkets, costing Rs. 300-600 for 5-10 GB plans
connectivity_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Ensure your phone is unlocked before purchasing a local SIM card
connectivity_tips._source_file: Mauritius/Copy of Mauritius All Inclusive Guide - Pradeep.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Best mobile providers are My.T (Mauritius Telecom), Emtel, and CHILI
Prepaid SIMs available at MRU airport or supermarkets, costing Rs. 300–600 for 5–10 GB plans
Ensure your phone is unlocked before purchasing a local SIM card
Best providers are My.T (Mauritius Telecom), Emtel, and CHILI with prepaid SIMs available at MRU airport or supermarkets
Cost is Rs. 300–600 for 5–10 GB plans. Ensure your phone is unlocked
Best providers are My.T (Mauritius Telecom), Emtel, and CHILI
Prepaid SIMs available at MRU airport or supermarkets
Cost: Rs. 300–600 for 5–10 GB plans
Ensure your phone is unlocked before travel
Both Mauritius Telecom and Emtel have counters at SSR International Airport
Passport required for SIM registration, setup takes 5 minutes
eSIM options available through Emtel and Chili (4G-only, data-focused)
Best mobile providers are My.T (Mauritius Telecom), Emtel, and CHILI. Prepaid SIMs available at MRU airport or supermarkets for Rs. 300–600 for 5–10 GB plans. Ensure your phone is unlocked
eSIM and regular SIMs available from Emtel or MyT (Mauritius Telecom)
Buy SIM at airport kiosk or supermarket (bring passport)
Excellent 4G coverage in both North & South
eSIM plans available via Airalo or Nomad eSIM (pre-download before trip)

transport_options.description: Primary transport throughout the trip unless stated otherwise

health_tips.tip: Mauritius is a safe tropical destination with no major health risks for travelers
Most visitors do not need special vaccines
Carry prescriptions in original packaging plus digital copy
Recommended: Routine shots + Hepatitis A, Typhoid (if planning street food)
